response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior individual contributor with genuine depth in developer tooling — he has built test runners, assertion libraries, CI/CD infrastructure, and delivered testing courses, which maps well to the engineering-productivity domain. However, this is a Level II people-management role requiring 2+ years of people management and 3+ years of technical leadership, and the resume shows an entirely IC-track career with no evidence of managing engineers, running one-on-ones, or owning team roadmaps. The domain fit is real but the core requirement of the role is unmet.
strengths: Deep developer-tools and test-infrastructure experience (test runner, assertion library, CI servers, device orchestration tooling)
18+ years of broad software development across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems
Demonstrated mentorship and knowledge-sharing (workshops, courses, code katas, conference talks)
concerns: No people-management experience — fails the 2-year management minimum
No demonstrated experience setting team priorities, performance expectations, or organizational roadmaps
Mobile experience is dated and app-level, not mobile-platform build/test ecosystem scale

key_factors.factor: Developer tooling and test automation depth
